title: Police Appeal for Public Assistance in Shooting Incident
content:
Hamilton,
09
April
2026
|
12:54
America/New_York
Police Appeal for Public Assistance in Shooting Incident
Hamilton Police are appealing to the public for assistance following the City’s latest shooting incident.
On Wednesday, April 8, 2026, at approximately 8:15 p.m., officers were called to a local Hospital to investigate the circumstances surrounding a 17-year-old female who had presented with a gunshot wound.
At this time, details surrounding the incident remain unclear and the parties involved are not cooperating with the investigation. Officers are continuing to gather information to determine where and how the shooting occurred.
The victim is currently recovering from her injuries.
Hamilton Police are asking anyone with information related to this incident to contact the Shooting Response Team at 905-546-4883.
To provide information anonymously, please contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit tips online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Police Investigating Death on Hamilton Waterfront
content:
Hamilton,
08
April
2026
|
19:00
America/New_York
Police Investigating Death on Hamilton Waterfront
Hamilton Police are currently investigating after a deceased person was located in the water near Harbourside Way and Discovery Drive.
At approximately 3:15 p.m. on April 8, 2026, police were called to the waterfront after a member of the public reported what appeared to be a person in the water. Responding officers attended and confirmed the individual was deceased. Detectives, along with the Marine Unit and Forensic Services, are on scene and continuing the investigation.
The identity of the individual has not yet been confirmed. The circumstances surrounding the death remain under investigation.
A police presence will remain in the area as officers continue their work. Members of the public may notice temporary restrictions along the waterfront.
Further information will be provided as it becomes available.

title: Police Make Arrest in Downtown Stabbing Incident
content:
Hamilton,
07
April
2026
|
13:11
America/New_York
Police Make Arrest in Downtown Stabbing Incident
Hamilton Police have arrested a 28-year-old male in connection with a stabbing that occurred in lower Hamilton.
On Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at approximately 3:30 a.m., police were called to the area of King Street and Victoria Avenue North following reports of a stabbing. Initial information indicated that a male had been injured and that the suspect remained in the vicinity.
Officers arrived on scene and located a 26-year-old male suffering from a stab wound to his torso. Police immediately provided first aid before the victim was transported to hospital with serious, but non-life-threatening injuries.
The suspect was located within minutes and taken into custody without incident.
The accused has been charged with:
Assault with a Weapon
Aggravated Assault
Police continue to investigate and are asking anyone with information that may assist to contact the on-duty Division 10 Staff Sergeant at 905-546-4725.
To provide information anonymously, please contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit tips online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

Hamilton Police Conclude Investigation into Reported Sexual Assault
The Hamilton Police Service Sex Crimes Unit has concluded its investigation into a reported abduction and sexual assault in the Hamilton East Mountain area during the late evening hours of March 28, 2026.
Through the course of the investigation, all involved parties have been identified. Investigators have determined that there is no ongoing threat to public safety in relation to this incident.
Hamilton Police recognize the impact that reports of this nature have on the community. We remain committed to thoroughly investigating all reports of sexual violence and supporting individuals who come forward.
There is no guaranteed way to prevent sexual assault. Sharing information about these incidents is intended to keep the community informed. Individuals are encouraged to trust their instincts. For confidential support, please contact the Sexual Assault Centre’s 24-hour support line at 905-525-4162 or visit
www.sacha.ca
, the Sexual Assault/Domestic Violence Care Centre at
www.hhsc.ca/sadv
, or the Centre de Santé Communautaire at
www.cschn.ca
.
To provide information anonymously, call Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit tips online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

Hamilton Police Investigating Abduction/ Sexual Assault
Hamilton Police is presently investigating an abduction and sexual assault which occurred on the Hamilton East Mountain area during the late evening hours of March 28, 2026.  The adult female victim was walking home when she was forced into a motor vehicle and driven to a separate location where she was sexually assaulted.  This appears to be a targeted incident.  The victim has been located, and has been medically assessed and released.
Police are actively looking for a lone suspect in relation to this incident.  The suspect is described as male black, late 20s to early 30s, medium build, 5’8” and was last observed wearing a black jacket.  The suspect was operating a motor vehicle but there is no description of this vehicle at the present time.
If you have any information that you believe could assist Police in this investigation, please contact Detective Paul Cottrill of the Sex Crimes Unit at 905-540-5544 or the Sex Crimes Unit non-emergency line at 905-540-5553.
We want all survivors of sexual violence to know that we believe them and we will respect their choice when and how they report the incident.
To provide information anonymously call Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
http://www.crimestoppershamilton.com
There is no guaranteed way to prevent sexual assault. Sharing the details of these assaults is intended to provide the community information, and people are encouraged to trust their instincts. For confidential support, please call the Sexual Assault Centre’s 24-hour support line at 905-525-4162 or connect at

title: Police Investigating Indecent Acts on Dundas Trails
content:
Hamilton,
26
March
2026
|
13:59
America/New_York
Police Investigating Indecent Acts on Dundas Trails
Hamilton Police are investigating two separate incidents involving indecent acts on walking trails in Dundas.
On March 21, 2026, police were called to a section of the rail trail near Lynden Road following a report of a male committing an indecent act. The victim reported that while walking along the trail, she observed a male standing approximately 100 metres away with his pants partially lowered, masturbating while staring in her direction.
On March 25, 2026, police received a second report of a similar incident on the trail. In this case, the victim observed a male nearby engaging in the same indecent act. Officers attended the area but were unable to locate the suspect.
In both incidents, the suspect is described as a white male wearing a black hoodie and white shoes.
Residents can expect to see an increased police presence in the area as the investigation continues.
Hamilton Police are reminding the public to follow these trail safety tips:
Stay on designated, well-maintained paths where possible
Avoid slopes and areas near the edge of the escarpment
Visit trails during daylight hours
Remain aware of your surroundings
Carry a fully charged cell phone
Travel in pairs or groups when possible
Anyone with information that may assist investigators is asked to contact the on-duty Division 30 Staff Sergeant at 905-546-3886.
To remain anonymous, please contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit a tip online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Hamilton Police Urge Caution Following Marketplace Robberies
content:
Hamilton,
25
March
2026
|
13:37
America/New_York
Hamilton Police Urge Caution Following Marketplace Robberies
download
Your browser does not support HTML5 video.
Buy and Sell Caution
Hamilton Police are reminding the public to exercise caution when buying and selling items through online marketplaces, following a recent robbery in the city.
On March 22, 2026, at approximately 9:30 p.m., police were called to a residence in east Hamilton for a reported robbery. A husband and wife had arranged the sale of an iPhone through Facebook Marketplace and agreed to meet the buyer at their home.
Upon arrival, the buyer was accompanied by two additional individuals. The couple observed that the group appeared uneasy. While one individual inspected the phone, the suspects suddenly fled the area on foot with the device. The husband pursued them, but the chase ended when he was sprayed with bear spray.
This incident marks the 14th marketplace-related robbery in Hamilton so far this year, with iPhones being the most commonly targeted item.
The Hamilton Police Service continues to investigate this incident and is urging residents to take precautions when conducting online transactions:
Meet in a public place:
Choose well-lit, busy locations. Hamilton Police Station 30 (400 Rymal Road East) offers a designated “Buy and Sell Zone” for safe transactions.
Check profiles carefully:
Be cautious of newly created accounts with little or no history, personal details, or transaction reviews.
Bring someone with you:
Attend meetups with a friend or inform someone of your meeting location and expected time.
Use secure payment methods:
Whenever possible, use electronic money transfers. Police have also seen incidents involving counterfeit cash.
Trust your instincts:
If something feels unsafe or suspicious, leave the situation immediately. There will always be other opportunities to buy or sell.
Anyone with information regarding this incident is asked to contact the Hamilton Police Service. Anonymous tips can be provided through Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Hamilton Police Warn About Fentanyl Gummies
content:
Hamilton,
06
September
2024
|
15:44
America/New_York
Hamilton Police Warn About Fentanyl Gummies
NewsRelease_SEP06_2024
Hamilton Police are issuing a warning after fentanyl-laced gummies were found during a recent warrant.
These dangerous substances, disguised as candy, pose a serious risk to public health and safety, particularly for youth who may unknowingly consume them.
What to Know:
Fentanyl is a powerful opioid, up to 100 times stronger than morphine, and even a small amount can cause a fatal overdose.
The fentanyl-laced gummies resemble regular candy, making them particularly dangerous for children and teens, as well as unsuspecting adults.
The gummies are often brightly colored and packaged in ways that could easily be mistaken for legitimate, store-bought products.
Warning Signs of Fentanyl Exposure:
Drowsiness or unresponsiveness
Difficulty breathing
Confusion or dizziness
Slow or weak pulse
If you or someone you know displays any of these symptoms after consuming a product resembling gummies or candy, call 911 immediately. Time is critical in preventing a potential overdose.
Hamilton Police urge parents, caregivers, and teachers to be vigilant and speak with children and young adults about the dangers of ingesting unknown substances.
What You Can Do:
Avoid accepting any candy or edibles from unknown sources.
Monitor what your children bring home, especially if they have been at parties or events.
Share this warning with friends and family to raise awareness.
Fentanyl is highly potent and unpredictable. Even a small amount can be deadly.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.
We appreciate your attention to this matter and thank you for your continued support in keeping our community safe.

title: Project Odeon Dismantles Significant Illegal Opioid Producers
content:
download
Your browser does not support HTML5 video.
POdeon_PressVideo_2023
Hamilton,
10
August
2023
|
13:01
America/New_York
Project Odeon Dismantles Significant Illegal Opioid Producers
Hamilton Police Service along with the Ontario Provincial Police, York Regional Police, Toronto Police Service and several other law enforcement agencies have dismantled a significant producer of fentanyl and other synthetic drugs in the Greater Hamilton and Toronto Area (GTHA).
Project Odeon began after police discovered a clandestine laboratory during an overdose death investigation in November 2021 at a residence on Hamilton’s Mountain. Investigators subsequently suspected a link between this death, an earlier overdose resulting in hospitalization and a death at a Toronto condominium. These all occurred within days of each other.
Opioid addiction has resulted in significant and far-reaching impacts on Hamilton. Like many other communities across the GTHA, Hamilton has been grappling with the devastating consequences of the opioid epidemic and declared a state of emergency in April 2023 related to homelessness, opioid addiction and mental health.
“As police, we have witnessed firsthand the devastating impact of opioids. These substances have not only led to a surge in addictions and overdoses but have also strained our resources as we respond to calls for service. Through Project Odeon, we have made a significant dent in the production of opioids and we believe this will have an impact on public safety in our community,” says Superintendent Marty Schulenberg.
From January 1 to July 30, 2023, there have been 606 incidents related to suspected opioid overdoses and 89-suspected drug-related deaths in Hamilton. These numbers have been steadily rising over the past five years.
“We are encouraged by the outcome of many long hours of investigatory work and thank our law enforcement partners for their continued commitment to reducing the production and distribution of drugs in Hamilton. Ultimately, the seizure of these opioids contributes to reducing the harms associated with opioid use for those who use substances, their family and friends, and the broader community,” says Julie Prieto, Director, Hamilton Public Health Services Epidemiology and Wellness Division.
By tracing the origins of some of the lab equipment, investigators were able to identify a company in the United States. This business sold three items of laboratory equipment to an Oshawa-based company. From here, investigators were able to piece together an extended drug network.
Warrants were executed in Hamilton, Mount Albert, Smithville, Toronto, Whitchurch-Stouffville, and Vaughan.
In total, investigators seized the following items:
An operational fentanyl drug lab at 6800 Sixteen Road, Smithville.
A dismantled fentanyl drug lab at 4057 Bethesda Road, Stouffville.
Approximately 3.5 tons of chemical byproduct from fentanyl production.
800 gallons of chemicals commonly used in the production of fentanyl
Lab equipment commonly used in the production of fentanyl
64.1 kg of illicit drugs, including 25.6 kg of fentanyl, 18 kg methamphetamine, 6 kg of ketamine
A loaded, Glock firearm and ammunition and four extended magazines
Over $350,000 of seized proceeds, including cars, jewelry, furniture and cash
ODEON Press Release Map_Version2
Download Media Kit

Download

The following 12 individuals have been charged through Project Odeon. To date, there are 48 criminal charges. Charges include possession for the purpose of trafficking, production of substance, proceeds of crime, firearm possession and conspiracy to commit an indictable offence.
Ly Duy Luan Do, 33-year-old from Hamilton
Charanpal Gill, 28-year-old from Stoney Creek
Jasanpal Gill, 22-year-old from Stoney Creek
Shkelqim Ibishi, 30-year-old from Hamilton,
Cindy Ly, 31-year-old  physician from Toronto
Elizabeth Milne, 33-year-old from Welland
Garry Moore, 45-year-old  from Smithville
Amanpreet Shergill, 30-year-old from Smithville
Ghais Sultani, 32-year-old from Hamilton
Akela Sultani, 59-year-old from Hamilton
Fais Sultani, 20-year-old  from Hamilton
Weeda Sultani, 19-year-old from Hamilton
The College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario is aware of the charges to Cindy Ly. There is also a Canada-wide warrant for 34-year-old Kien Trung Hoang from Toronto.
“Dismantling a clan lab requires up to 50 personnel from police, fire, EMS, the Ontario Fire Marshall and Health Canada. Through Project ODEON, we were able to assist the Hamilton Police Service in safely dismantling not one, but two, clandestine labs. This investigation has had a significant impact on reducing harms and improving public safety,” said Ontario Provincial Police Detective Superintendent Paula Milne.
Crime knows no borders. Law enforcement agencies continue to work cooperatively to share information to combat illegal activity and create safer communities. Thank you to the Canadian Border Services Agency, Canadian Intelligence Service Ontario, Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (FINTRAC), Guelph Police Service, Niagara Regional Police Service, Ontario Provincial Police, Public Prosecution Service of Canada (PPSC), Toronto Police Service and York Regional Police Service for supporting Project Odeon.
If you have any information that you believe could assist police with the investigation into these crimes, please contact Detective Eric Hrab at 905-546-6340.
To provide information anonymously call Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at www.crimestoppershamilton.com

title: Hamilton Police Address Increase in Youth Violence
content:
06
March
2026
|
11:49
America/New_York
Hamilton Police Address Increase in Youth Violence
Launch of Project Breathe Easy
Bear Spray - Info session
Hamilton Police Service is advising the community of a concerning increase in youth-related violent incidents, including a rise in the misuse of bear spray during altercations.
Over the past several months, officers have responded to a number of incidents involving young persons engaged in assaults, group conflicts, and weapons-related offences. Of particular concern is the growing trend of youth carrying and deploying bear spray in disputes.
Since March 3, police have responded to five incidents, with three youths facing charges.
Bear spray-related incidents have steadily increased each year between 2021 and 2025, representing a 115 per cent rise over the past five years. Youth aged 10 to 19 account for approximately 63 per cent of identified offenders. The most frequently reported offence involving bear spray is assault with a weapon.
Bear spray is designed as a wildlife deterrent and contains a highly concentrated irritant. When discharged in public spaces such as schools, malls, transit, or residential areas, it can cause significant respiratory distress, temporary blindness, panic, and serious safety risks to bystanders. When used against another person, bear spray is considered a weapon under the Criminal Code and may result in criminal charges.
While most youth in Hamilton make positive choices and contribute meaningfully to our community, the recent increase in violence highlights the need for proactive education, prevention, and enforcement.
In response to this trend, Hamilton Police have launched Project Breathe Easy, a targeted initiative aimed at reducing bear spray-related violence in the community.
Project Breathe Easy includes increased enforcement and high-visibility patrols in identified hotspot areas, proactive engagement with youth, compliance checks at retailers selling bear spray to ensure adherence to applicable legislation and responsible sales practices and ongoing monitoring and intelligence-led response to emerging concerns. The initiative combines enforcement with education to address both access and misuse.
“We don’t see a lot of bears walking around Hamilton. The reality is, we don’t have a bear problem — we have a youth safety problem that needs to be addressed,” says Chief Frank Bergen. “We’re not here to alarm families — we’re here to support them. At the same time, we are taking enforcement action where necessary to keep our community safe.”
Community Information Sessions
In addition to enforcement efforts, Hamilton Police are hosting a series of community information sessions aimed at parents and caregivers.
The sessions will focus on:
Current youth violence trends
The risks and legal implications of bear spray misuse
Warning signs parents should be aware of
How social media contributes to conflict escalation
Strategies for prevention and early intervention
Available community and mental health resources
Both in-person and virtual sessions will be offered to ensure accessibility for families across Hamilton. The virtual session will include breakout rooms in Arabic, Urdu, and French to support inclusive community dialogue.
In-Person Session

Hamilton Police emphasize that youth violence prevention requires collaboration between families, schools, community organizations, retailers, and law enforcement.
Early conversations at home, awareness of peer dynamics and online activity, and engagement in positive activities are among the strongest protective factors for young people.
“These sessions are about education, prevention, and accountability,” adds Inspector Frank Miscione. “We want parents to feel informed and supported, and we want youth to understand there are real consequences when weapons are brought into conflicts.”
Members of the community are encouraged to attend and participate in the discussions.
For more information, please visit
